author: @Nakasupy
text: Hi Robert: I think the best design would be a combination of your two. The one with the feed back from the horizontal/vertical intersection, but with the feed on the 45 degree angle. I believe it would burn hotter due to the fuel having a longer fire chamber due to the fuel coming in at the angle, and the extra horizontal length. As well more heat would be transfered, again, due to the extra length, and It wood still be self feeding with the angle. I just don't see that there would be any detrimental effect as to whether the feed chamber is 45, or 90 degrees. In effect, it's just a hole in a pipe, either way. It would be fantastic to see the results of that, then that design incorporated into a heater design, with the vertical chimney as the insulated gasification chamber. Cheers!

author: @g0fvt
text: Robert, an anecdote for you, a friend of a friend built a stereo audio amplifier with separate mains transformers  for each channel (toroidal) he did the chassis work himself. He powered up one channel and found the other was not totally dead. Embarrassingly he contacted the transformer manufacturer to complain that one of them was producing output with no input. If you have not guessed the issue was the mounting method, he effectively had created a single turn coupling between the two of them.

author: @BarneyKlimeck
text:
I beleive zero point energy is possible and sooner or later someone will  make a generator so efficient that it will be able to produce enough surplus energy that it could run itself plus a substantial surplus to run a modern home for example. I believe it will obey the known laws of thermodynamics and be simple enough that most people could replicate it. 
Remember when  it was said that the speed of sound couldn't be beat unti it was. Just because I can't do it doesn't mean somebody won't.  The same goes for wireless electric transfer stations at each home in America.   Our electric grids infrastructure is old and is beginning to need major infrastructure updates to just maintain todays standards. The impossible is only impossible until it's not. Why not showcase some of the new technology coming from the search of zero point energy  and  wireless power transmission.

text: With my curiosity level raised, I went across and began watching the video by Technologia Oculta. Part way through, after seeing how many field poles there are in the alternator, I Googled the AC frequency of car alternators. They will obviously vary because the car engine runs at varying RPM but a figure of between 4000 to 6000 Hz seemed to be a common figure quoted, with the alternator running at about 6000 RPM. They say that their machine runs at about 3000 RPM, at that speed, the alternator would be generating an AC voltage at somewhere around 2000-3000Hz. The motor is designed to run from a 50Hz supply, apart from the fact it probably wouldn't run from a 2000Hz supply, if it did it would try to rotate at considerably more than 3000 RPM. These are only rough 'back of a cigarette packet' numbers but you can see that the figures they quote are far from anything real.

author: @MrAnderson4509
text:
speaking generally and not directly referencing the particular item that you're talking about, but more of a general area of concept, That is to say some thing for nothing.

You make some great points Robert, I do enjoy watching some of those builds, most of the time I watch them to see if anybody re-created what I built when I was 13, 

To this day I have not seen exactly the configuration, I did collect some parts to see if I could re-create the thing, because it sticks in my mind some of the weird things that It did, along with the traveling surface frost that traveld over the surface of the motor housing of the old GE motor when it spun up to fly apart speed ( it scared me because I thought she was going to come apart as I knew it was rated for 1,800 rpm) I was going to try and re-create mine about four years ago but I had some turmoil and theft going on in my life's of tools and materials. I really should build mine and test it proper, that is if I can ever reproduce my setup again, Unfortunately my setup was stolen in the night by a neighbor and brought to a physics prof at the University of Minnesota were one of the motors was damaged and the other motor was replaced because they thought it was supposedly damaged, that neighbor, Randy page, had no business going into our garage and taking the thing, I could've learned a lot by investigating why it did the things that it did, But no some greedy pig had to interfere and then break the damn thing (The damn thing being a device that appeared to give you something for nothing, but that I suspected was merely storing the energy I put into it, ever too many variables involved in the stupid thing to make assumptions without testing, like the fact that I had it hooked up to the mains to start it but that it did not start without me wrapping a cord around the motor shaft of one of the motors and starting it by hand like a lawnmower, a bit difficult to calculate energy is in the goofy thing, The goal in building the thing had nothing to do with perpetual motion pursuit, in fact it was actually designed for a model wind tunnel, but when I unplugged it after trying to shut it off with the switch, and it wouldn't stop running while it was trying to attack me with its exposed fan blades, understandably I wanted to investigate it LMAO) unfortunately the simpleton next door sot next-door and thought he had to steal it.

my first An immediate summation of what I had discovered was just something of high-efficiency, as it did run for three days after I restarted it without that rotating death machine attached to the shaft of one of the motors. 

please understand though I do get a great chuckle out of all those "magical set ups", especially when some of them is magnetics under a table or some other trickery, but agreeably some of these that I have seen are just huge pile of buffalo done, especially that one that uses the spark plugs OMG! lmao

I almost believed it for a sec..  Except that I know for a fact from my car audio days that a car alternator only produces around 1 kw MAX like max max.. author: @kenrowe167
text: Hi Rob. If we discount the costs of equipment production etc. then, of course, there is such a thing as free energy. Solar engergy is free; wind, hydro and wave energy are free. If we double the number of solar panels, it doesn't deplete the sun by even one hydrogen atom. But conversion efficiency is something quite different. Photovotaics are around 23/24% efficient. The best wind turbine we can build can't beat the Betz limit of 16/27.But no matter how much our technologies improve, one thing remains absolute. We simply cannot get more energy out of an apparatus than we put in. So I am not unsure. I am quite happy to bet the house when I say that their claims for their machine are pure bunkum!!

author: @ryanjamesloyd6733
text:
I ran across that yesterday- and the one that they go back through and try to assure everyone it does work.  (addendum: They've expanded to the same video on Another channel, which I also just ran across, I suspect you're right about the ad revenue being the motive here)  
Though I'm extremely skeptical of it I think this particular idea is attractive as it seems like there Might be a way to find a hack using a small motor to turn very strong magnets fast enough within coils to generate more than it takes to run (as the energy it takes to physically Move the magnets isn't directly related to the output of the generator, as that's more determined by the strength of the magnetic fields, size of the coils, and speed as you say- back emf notwithstanding)- 
And not in an Overunity sort of sense, but in a clever loophole sense, like the sail-car that can go straight downwind faster than the wind is blowing (which was considered impossible until recently).

We exploit all kinds of things to get more out of them than the same amount of energy applied (by us) directly could achieve, (like a heat pump, vs a standard electric heater- it's the compressed refrigerant that does the work of moving more heat than a resistance coil could put out for the same input).  And we accept these things because obviously they work- but there's a whole lot of resistance to those Looking for the clever hacks.  

I dunno.  I think if somebody figures this one out, it will be a low torque- high speed design (which has been in the back of my head since your washing machine wind turbine series)

author: @shaneroper5470
text:
Zero point energy does exist and is quite possible.  People don't realize what generators actually do.  The word "generator" is misguiding,  because that's not what they do at all.  The arc firm motor,  is basically an inside out magnet.  Magnets on the outside,  and copper windings in the inside.  You have to fields. Magnetism and dialectricity.  The spinning action of the motor agitating these two fields,  allows electricity to be manifest.  Electricity is a HYBRID OF Dialectricity and Magnetism.  
Zero point energy is very possible.  It's just a matter of getting the geometry right,  and it could be achieved with no moving parts.

author: @mac-qt3wd
text: Forgive me for asking as its one thing I've never tried but can't you just gear something up massively? It doesn't take alot of current to spin a dc motor at full speed hooked up to a battery, try a battery drill motor for example, they have a bit of torque. Put a wheel and belt on then put one a hundred times bigger on a wind permanent magnet alternator and see what comes out?

replies.author: @1967spark
replies.text: Gears are the mechanical equivalent of transformers. They can change the speed of rotation (and can also change the direction) but the output power available will be less than the input power.  Speed isn't power.

replies.author: @mac-qt3wd
replies.text: @1967spark the faster you turn a magnet the more power you get, more rpms equals more power, thats fairly common knowledge

replies.author: @mac-qt3wd
replies.text: @1967spark otherwise whats the point of the big wind tubines gearing up at a ratio between 90-100?

replies.author: @1967spark
replies.text:
@mac-qt3wd to have the generator turn at an optimum speed. But the gear box does not increase the power output, it's just a factor in the design efficiency.
The wind speed and diameter of the blades are the principle values for the power available.

replies.author: @1967spark
replies.text: @mac-qt3wd The faster you go the more voltage. Not power.

author: @markashworth6293
text: I tried to build the alternator generator system..... problem being an alternator needs to run at 3000 rpm to self excite (with a simple modification unless you glue neomydium magnets in there .... and push out a 12v output to power the motor and other stuff you want to power, the motor wasnt ideally matched to the alternator thus having losses and in the end seems to be an impossible idea and i think the system will slow down and stop eventually through the losses, presumablly the more you load this thing with an inverter more losses are introduced, and you are right that you should call all of these videos a fake...... thanks for the enlightenment on how much you can get for a viral video!

author: @EgonSorensen
text:
"Heavier Than Air Flight Is Impossible" — Lord Kelvin, 1895. 
"I have not the smallest molecule of faith in aerial navigation other than ballooning, or of the expectation of good results from any of the trials we heard of. So you will understand that I would not care to be a member of the Aeronautical Society." — Lord Kelvin, 1896

Civilization has since concluded Lord Kelvin was wrong - but strangely enough a majority of the electric power generated is still being produced by steam turbines - in 2022... 126 years later.  Fake or not, Lord Kelvin with his many Honours was wrong - and wrong on other things as well. 

Modern science has become an experts playing ground, with highly specific research and ecco chambers cheering on for more of the same :ø(
Imho society needs more generalists than specialists, especially those not afraid of taking the risk of ridicule and failure. The winnings would be :ø)

replies.author: @1967spark
replies.text:
Not sure that you should equate what one person said (with a limited amount of data) with the knowledge of a larger number of people.

If someone ever does come up with a "free energy" device they will know exactly where that energy is coming from and be able to demonstrate it clearly.

replies.author: @VinoVeritas_
replies.text: So how did Lord Kelvin account for all the birds he would have seen that contradicted that quote? I doubt that quotation is even real.

replies.text: there does seem to be a lack of being prepared to be wrong that's for sure

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: @VinoVeritas_ Excellent point! Seems like Lord Kelvin was not as observant as I thought he was. :)

author: @TheBaconWizard
text: Plenty of charlatons can show you a "working" thing in-person, and it's less effort to arrange than any stage magic. I'd definitely stick with whether or not they can tell YOU how to build one that works. They should be very keen to do-so.

author: @VirideSoryuLangley
text: Well, I'm not a scientist or an inventor, but I've heard that coils need to be built a certain way, with specific measurements, otherwise they won't work. It's a big issue for PEMF machines, apparently. So I don't know, maybe you need to build it exactly the way they did, in order to get the same effect. Just my 2 cents.

replies.author: @TheBaconWizard
replies.text: Rob is being extremely generous, he has plenty of very good reasons to believe it doesn't work at-all as anybody else who is educated in scientific fields of endeaver will do. But we should all be prepared to be proven wrong if such people are prepared to do it properly, but not hold our breath. Such things have been proposed over, and over and over again, for decades and decades without a single one actually working. The principles at work are FAR below degree-level.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the basic principle should still work mate irrespective of the coil size or arrangement - in fact the little arrangement did work - a few nanowatts were produced as I said in the video  - but no where near what is claimed. That is not a surprise the ability of a coil to generate is based on number of turns, magnetic field strength and speed that the magnetic field cuts the coils - all of these were very small in. my replication and in the original - so you would expect tiny tiny tiny amounts of power and that's what you get - not the watts they were claiming

author: @solidideas6756
text: You have to try this. Ex take two 3v battery in series and one 3v battery. Connect the ground on the singel battery with the one ground in the batteries in series.
